message.content:
You are resuming a crashed agent session on the bim-ai repo
  (/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai). bim-ai is a browser-based BIM authoring
  tool (React + TypeScript + Three.js, Vite, Vitest).

  REPO ORIENTATION (WP-B relevant paths):
  - packages/web/src/tools/toolRegistry.ts — ToolId union + TOOL_REGISTRY
  - packages/web/src/tools/toolGrammar.ts — per-tool grammar
  - packages/web/src/plan/moveTool.ts — reference pattern for tool grammar
  - packages/web/src/plan/arrayTool.ts — array math helpers (already done)
  - packages/web/src/plan/createSimilar.ts — already done
  - packages/web/src/plan/joinGeometry.ts — command helpers already done
  - packages/web/src/plan/gripProtocol.ts — grip interaction protocol
  - packages/web/src/plan/GripLayer.tsx — grip rendering
  - packages/web/src/clipboard/copyToLevels.ts — paste-to-levels command (done)
  - packages/web/src/clipboard/clipboardStore.ts — clipboard Zustand store
  - packages/web/src/clipboard/copyPaste.ts — Ctrl+C/V logic
  - packages/web/src/groups/groupTypes.ts — GroupDefinition, GroupInstance types (done)
  - packages/web/src/groups/groupCommands.ts — pure group command logic (done)
  - packages/web/src/state/ — Zustand store slices

  Architecture:
  - Semantic commands: onSemanticCommand({ type: 'moveElement', elementId, deltaXMm, deltaYMm }). Study moveTool.ts.
  - Store: access project model via useBimStore. Mutations go through commandQueue.ts.
  - Tests: co-located *.test.ts, run with pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web.
  - Prettier runs automatically.

  WHAT WAS DONE BEFORE THE CRASH:
  - B4 Create Similar: DONE — plan/createSimilar.ts + tests
  - B5 Array math helpers: PARTIAL — plan/arrayTool.ts + tests done; PlanCanvas wiring and full grammar pending
  - B7 Join/Unjoin helpers: PARTIAL — plan/joinGeometry.ts + tests done; toolbar UI wiring pending
  - B2 Group types + commands: PARTIAL — groups/groupTypes.ts, groups/groupCommands.ts, groups/groupCommands.test.ts done (pure logic); zero UI wiring
  - B3 copyToLevels command: PARTIAL — clipboard/copyToLevels.ts + copyToLevels.test.ts done; dialog UI pending
  - ToolId scaffold committed: 'scale', 'array', 'place-group' are in the ToolId union in toolRegistry.ts

  Run first: git pull --rebase origin main

  WHAT STILL NEEDS TO BE DONE:

  B1 — Scale Tool grammar + command handler
  'scale' ToolId exists. Add:
  1. Grammar in tools/scale.ts: after elements selected, activate Scale → pick origin point → type numeric factor + Enter OR graphical two-click reference (from→to distance defines scale ratio) → live canvas
   preview
  2. Command handler for scaleElement: walls scale endpoint coords relative to origin; family instances scale insertion point + size params
  3. Tests: scale a wall from origin, verify endpoints; scale a door instance

  B2 — Model Groups UI wiring
  Pure logic in groups/ is done. Wire into the app:
  1. "Create Group" action in selection toolbar when ≥2 elements are selected — prompts for name, dispatches createGroup command
  2. 'place-group' tool grammar — options bar shows dropdown of group definitions; click places a group_instance
  3. Plan renderer plan/groupInstanceRender.ts — renders grouped elements at transformed positions; dashed rectangle boundary when selected
  4. 3D renderer viewport/groupInstance3d.ts — transforms definition geometry by instance insertion/rotation
  5. Group edit mode — "Edit Group" button ghosts non-group elements, only members editable; "Finish Editing" updates all instances
  6. Inspector on group_instance: name, rotation, Edit Group button, Ungroup button, Select All Instances button
  7. Project browser "Groups" subtree listing definitions + instance counts
  Tests: create group from 2 walls, place 2nd instance, verify both render, ungroup one, verify elements are independent

  B3 — Paste to Levels dialog
  copyToLevels.ts command exists. Wire the UI:
  - "Copy to Levels…" button in selection toolbar → level picker modal with checkboxes → dispatch copyToLevels
  - Also wire Ctrl+C on selection → store IDs in clipboardStore; verify Ctrl+V pastes at cursor (check clipboard/copyPaste.ts)

  B5 — Array tool PlanCanvas wiring
  Math helpers exist. Still needed:
  - Full grammar in tools/array.ts (check if it exists first):
    - Linear: select → activate → pick start → pick end → type count → Enter. Options bar: Number field, "Move To: 2nd / Last" toggle.
    - Radial: options bar toggle → pick centre → type angle + count → Enter
    - Live canvas preview
  - Tests: linear array of 3 columns at 5000mm, verify positions

  B6 — Selection Filter Dialog (not started)
  - plan/selectionFilter.tsx: modal listing category counts (Walls 6, Doors 3…), each row with checkbox; unchecking deselects those elements
  - "Filter" chip in selection toolbar showing "N elements selected"
  - deselectByCategory action dispatched from the dialog
  - "Select All Instances in Project" context action: deselects all, re-selects every element sharing same typeId

  B7 — Join/Unjoin toolbar UI
  joinGeometry.ts helpers exist. Still needed:
  - "Join" selection action (when ≥2 solid elements selected): dispatches { type: 'joinGeometry', elementId1, elementId2 }
  - "Unjoin" selection action: clears the join
  - Small "join" glyph rendered at the junction in plan view when joined

  B8 — Pin/Unpin toolbar
  pinUnpin.ts helpers + PN cheatsheet shortcut exist. Still needed:
  - PN keyboard shortcut wired to pin all selected elements (currently in cheatsheet but not dispatching)
  - "Unpin All" button in selection toolbar
  - Padlock icon glyph in plan canvas for pinned elements (check PlanCanvas.padlock.test.tsx for what's expected)

  RULES:
  - git pull --rebase origin main before every major sub-task
  - Commit + push after each completed sub-task
  - Do NOT touch stair/ramp, annotation/dimension, export, phase, structural, or massing files
  - toolRegistry.ts is shared — always rebase before editing
  - pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web before each push
  - Update spec/revit-parity/revit2026-parity-tracker.md as you complete items

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# Memory Index
2	
3	- [Agent prompt quality](feedback_agent_prompts.md) — Worker agents are 4x slower with loose "find X" prompts vs tight old_string/new_string prompts; see AGENTS.md for full pattern
4	- [Track before executing big refactors](feedback_track_before_executing_refactors.md) — When an audit surfaces several multi-hour refactors, write a tracker MD first; confirm execution scope only after
5	- [Code-quality tracker location](reference_code_quality_tracker.md) — Architectural debt lives in `spec/code-quality-tracker.md`, separate from the workpackage-master-tracker; prod-readiness items are out of scope there
6	- [v3 design emphasis](project_v3_design_emphasis.md) — v3 tracker (post-2026-05-08) treats design + UX as a load-bearing pillar; "software soll nach Architektur wirken," not engineering
7	- [Gemini video pattern](reference_gemini_video_pattern.md) — `~/repos/hof-video-generation-mvp` has a working google-genai SDK + YouTube-URL ingestion pattern for any video-analysis task
8	- [No markdown tables](feedback_formatting.md) — terminal renders tables/hyphenated bold as strikethrough; use plain lists only

# Memory Index

- [Agent prompt quality](feedback_agent_prompts.md) — Worker agents are 4x slower with loose "find X" prompts vs tight old_string/new_string prompts; see AGENTS.md for full pattern
- [Track before executing big refactors](feedback_track_before_executing_refactors.md) — When an audit surfaces several multi-hour refactors, write a tracker MD first; confirm execution scope only after
- [Code-quality tracker location](reference_code_quality_tracker.md) — Architectural debt lives in `spec/code-quality-tracker.md`, separate from the workpackage-master-tracker; prod-readiness items are out of scope there
- [v3 design emphasis](project_v3_design_emphasis.md) — v3 tracker (post-2026-05-08) treats design + UX as a load-bearing pillar; "software soll nach Architektur wirken," not engineering
- [Gemini video pattern](reference_gemini_video_pattern.md) — `~/repos/hof-video-generation-mvp` has a working google-genai SDK + YouTube-URL ingestion pattern for any video-analysis task
- [No markdown tables](feedback_formatting.md) — terminal renders tables/hyphenated bold as strikethrough; use plain lists only

import type {
  Element,
  LensMode,
  ModelDelta,
  PerspectiveId,
  Snapshot,
  Violation,
  WorkspaceLayoutPreset,
} from '@bim-ai/core';

import type { FamilyDefinition } from '../families/types';

import type { WallLocationLine } from '../tools/toolGrammar';
import type { PlanPresentationPreset } from '../plan/symbology';
import type {
  PlanProjectionPrimitivesV1Wire,
  PlanCategoryGraphicHintsV0Wire,
  PlanRoomColorLegendRow,

1	/**
2	 * Per-tool grammar — spec §16.4 / §16.5.
3	 *
4	 * Each tool's interaction model is captured here as plain data so the
5	 * tool can be unit-tested without DOM. The shape is consistent across
6	 * tools so the canvas can switch on `kind` and dispatch input.
7	 */
8	
9	export type { WallLocationLine } from '@bim-ai/core';
10	import type { WallLocationLine } from '@bim-ai/core';
11	
12	/* ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
13	/* EDT-06 — Tool grammar polish (Chain / Multiple / Tag-on-Place /        */
14	/*           Numeric input). The canvas wires the per-tool reducers in    */
15	/*           this file plus a top-level `ToolGrammarModifiers` block      */
16	/*           that feeds the Options Bar.                                  */
17	/* ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
18	
19	/** Modifiers that the canvas Options Bar exposes to the user. */
20	export interface ToolGrammarModifiers {
21	  /** Place Wall continues from the last endpoint until Esc / different tool. */
22	  chainable: boolean;
23	  /** Insert Door / Window stays in tool until Esc; otherwise exits after first placement. */
24	  multipleable: boolean;
25	  /** During wall / door / window placement, auto-place a tag of the configured family. */
26	  tagOnPlace: { enabled: boolean; tagFamilyId?: string };
27	  /** Numeric input mode: typing a digit while drawing pops a numeric input field. */
28	  numericInputActive: boolean;
29	  /**
30	   * Wall draw offset — when non-zero, the wall baseline is drawn offset from
31	   * the cursor by this many mm (positive = left side of draw direction).
32	   * F-042 parity. Default 0.
33	   */
34	  wallDrawOffsetMm: number;
35	  /**
36	   * Wall draw radius — when non-null, consecutive wall segments get a curved
37	   * corner fillet of this radius (mm). F-043 parity. Default null (sharp corners).
38	   */
39	  wallDrawRadiusMm: number | null;
40	}
41	
42	export function defaultToolGrammarModifiers(): ToolGrammarModifiers {
43	  return {
44	    chainable: true,
45	    multipleable: false,
46	    tagOnPlace: { enabled: false },
47	    numericInputActive: false,
48	    wallDrawOffsetMm: 0,
49	    wallDrawRadiusMm: null,
50	  };
51	}
52	
53	/** A tool's static capability set — drives which Options Bar toggles appear. */
54	export interface ToolCapabilities {
55	  chainable: boolean;
56	  multipleable: boolean;
57	  tagOnPlace: boolean;
58	  /** Whether the tool supports typing a numeric distance while drawing. */
59	  numericInput: boolean;
60	}
61

187	  ramp: { chainable: false, multipleable: false, tagOnPlace: false, numericInput: true },
188	};
189	
190	/**
191	 * Numeric-input field state — appears at the cursor while a numeric-capable
192	 * tool is mid-draw and the user types a digit. `axis` toggles on Tab so a
193	 * second number can drive the perpendicular direction.
194	 */
195	export interface NumericInputState {
196	  active: boolean;
197	  value: string;
198	  axis: 'primary' | 'perpendicular';
199	}
200	
201	export function initialNumericInputState(): NumericInputState {
202	  return { active: false, value: '', axis: 'primary' };
203	}
204	
205	export type NumericInputEvent =
206	  | { kind: 'start'; firstDigit: string }
207	  | { kind: 'append'; digit: string }
208	  | { kind: 'backspace' }
209	  | { kind: 'tab-axis' }
210	  | { kind: 'commit' }
211	  | { kind: 'cancel' };
212	
213	export function reduceNumericInput(
214	  state: NumericInputState,
215	  event: NumericInputEvent,
216	): NumericInputState {
217	  switch (event.kind) {
218	    case 'start':
219	      return { active: true, value: event.firstDigit, axis: 'primary' };
220	    case 'append':
221	      if (!state.active) return state;
222	      return { ...state, value: state.value + event.digit };
223	    case 'backspace':
224	      if (!state.active) return state;
225	      return { ...state, value: state.value.slice(0, -1) };
226	    case 'tab-axis':
227	      if (!state.active) return state;
228	      return {
229	        ...state,
230	        axis: state.axis === 'primary' ? 'perpendicular' : 'primary',
231	      };
232	    case 'commit':
233	    case 'cancel':
234	      return initialNumericInputState();
235	  }
236	}
237

239	  | 'wall'
240	  | 'door'

} from '@bim-ai/icons';

/**
 * BIM AI chrome icon set — the single import surface for icon usage outside
 * the canvas. BIM-specific and generic chrome icons both come from
 * `@bim-ai/icons`.
 *
 * Default visual rules (spec §10):
 *   - Default size 16 px in dense chrome; 18 px in tool palette; 20 px in
 *     TopBar. Pass `size` prop to override.
 *   - Default stroke width 1.5; selected state 2.0.
 *   - Color inherits `currentColor` so theme tokens drive it.
 *
 * Every icon-only button must pair its icon with `aria-label` (spec §22).
 */

export type BimIconComponent = ComponentType<
  SVGAttributes<SVGSVGElement> & {
    size?: number | string;
    strokeWidth?: number | string;
    absoluteStrokeWidth?: boolean;
  }
>;

/** @deprecated use StairIcon from @bim-ai/icons. Kept for test backwards compat. */
export { StairIcon as StairsIcon } from '@bim-ai/icons';

/** Spec §10.1 "concept → icon" registry. Single source of truth for chrome
 * icons. Tools / shell / dialogs read from here so the assignments can be
 * audited as a unit. */
export const Icons: Record<string, BimIconComponent> = {
  // Selection & manipulation
  select: SelectIcon,

  // Modify tools
  align: AlignIcon,
  copy: CopyIcon,
  measure: MeasureBetweenIcon,
  split: SplitIcon,
  trim: TrimExtendIcon,
  'wall-join': WallJoinsIcon,
  'wall-opening': OpeningIcon,
  shaft: ShaftOpeningIcon,
  mirror: MirrorIcon,
  move: MoveIcon,
  rotate: RotateIcon,

  // Drawing tools — all now BIM-native from @bim-ai/icons
  wall: WallIcon,
  door: DoorIcon,
  window: WindowIcon,
  floor: FloorIcon,
  roof: RoofIcon,
  stair: StairIcon,
  railing: RailingIcon,
  room: RoomIcon,
  dimension: DimensionIcon,
  section: SectionIcon,
  tag: TagIcon,
  curtainWall: CurtainWallIcon,
  column: ColumnIcon,
  beam: BeamIcon,
  ceiling: CeilingIcon,
  duct: DuctRectIcon,
  pipe: PipeIcon,
  cableTray: CableTrayIcon,
  mepEquipment: MechanicalEquipmentIcon,
  fixture: PlumbingFixtureIcon,
  mepTerminal: LightingFixtureIcon,

  // Views & documentation — BIM-native
  planView: PlanViewIcon,
  sectionView: SectionViewIcon,
  elevationView: ElevationViewIcon,
  orbitView: OrbitViewIcon,
  sheet: SheetIcon,
  schedule: ScheduleViewIcon,
  callout: CalloutIcon,
  viewpoint: ViewpointIcon,
  sectionBox: SectionBoxIcon,
  gridLine: GridLineIcon,
  level: LevelIcon,
  detailLine: DetailLineIcon,

  // Organization & data — BIM-native
  family: FamilyIcon,
  familyType: FamilyTypeIcon,
  group: GroupIcon,
  assembly: AssemblyIcon,
  linkedModel: LinkedModelIcon,
  material: MaterialIcon,
  wallLayer: WallLayerIcon,
  phase: PhaseIcon,

  // Coordination — BIM-native
  issue: IssueIcon,
  clash: ClashIcon,
  validationRule: ValidationRuleIcon,
  deviation: DeviationIcon,
  comment: RFIIcon,

export const AUTHORING_COMMAND_CONTRACTS = {
  select: contract(
    'select',
    'review',
    ['plan', '3d', 'section', 'sheet', 'schedule'],
    'Selection cursor with hover hit feedback and no model mutation.',
    'selection-only',
  ),
  query: contract(
    'query',
    'review',
    ['plan'],
    'Hover/click imported underlay geometry and report the source primitive.',
    'stay-active',
    ['visible-import-underlay'],
  ),
  wall: contract(
    'wall',
    'line',
    ['plan', '3d'],
    'Two-click wall center/location line preview or picked floor/CAD line preview; committed payload must match preview endpoints.',
    'remain-active-while-looping',
    [],
    ['type', 'level', 'height', 'location-line', 'chain', 'flip', 'pick-lines'],
  ),
  door: contract(
    'door',
    'hosted',
    ['plan', '3d'],
    'Host-wall preview with swing, rough opening, and conflict state before click.',

130	
131	  it('covers every modal/dialog command with canonical trigger ownership — UX-RISK-005', () => {
132	    const dialogTriggers = new Map<string, readonly string[]>([
133	      ['project.restore-snapshot', ['cmd-k', 'primary-sidebar']],
134	      ['project.share-presentation', ['cmd-k', 'header']],
135	      ['project.manage-links', ['cmd-k', 'primary-sidebar']],
136	      ['project.import.ifc', ['cmd-k', 'primary-sidebar']],
137	      ['project.import.dxf', ['cmd-k', 'primary-sidebar']],
138	      ['library.open-family', ['cmd-k', 'ribbon', 'secondary-sidebar']],
139	      ['library.open-material-browser', ['cmd-k', 'primary-sidebar', 'element-sidebar']],
140	      ['library.open-appearance-asset-browser', ['cmd-k', 'primary-sidebar', 'element-sidebar']],
141	      ['help.keyboard-shortcuts', ['cmd-k']],
142	      ['help.replay-onboarding-tour', ['cmd-k', 'primary-sidebar']],
143	      ['advisor.open', ['cmd-k', 'footer']],
144	      ['jobs.open', ['cmd-k', 'footer']],
145	      ['milestone.open', ['cmd-k', 'primary-sidebar']],
146	      ['advisor.apply-first-fix', ['cmd-k', 'dialog']],
147	      ['sheet.export-share', ['cmd-k', 'ribbon', 'header']],
148	      ['visibility.plan.graphics', ['cmd-k', 'ribbon', 'secondary-sidebar']],
149	      ['clipboard.paste-to-levels', ['cmd-k']],
150	    ] as const);
151	    const modalCapabilities = getAllCommandCapabilities()
152	      .filter((capability) => capability.executionSurface === 'modal')
153	      .sort((a, b) => a.id.localeCompare(b.id));
154	
155	    expect(modalCapabilities.map((capability) => capability.id)).toEqual(
156	      [...dialogTriggers.keys()].sort(),
157	    );

content: Conversation compacted

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user asked to resume a crashed agent session working on WP-B items in the bim-ai repository — a browser-based BIM authoring tool (React + TypeScript + Three.js, Vite, Vitest). The task involves implementing several Revit-parity work package B items, picking up from partial work done in the previous session that crashed.

   Rules specified by the user:
   - `git pull --rebase origin main` before every major sub-task
   - Commit + push after each completed sub-task
   - Do NOT touch stair/ramp, annotation/dimension, export, phase, structural, or massing files
   - `toolRegistry.ts` is shared — always rebase before editing
   - `pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web` before each push
   - Update `spec/revit-parity/revit2026-parity-tracker.md` as items complete

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **bim-ai stack**: React + TypeScript + Three.js, Vite, Vitest, pnpm monorepo
   - **ToolId union type**: All tool IDs in `toolRegistry.ts`; 'scale', 'array', 'place-group' were already scaffolded
   - **AuthoringCommandContract**: Every tool in the registry must have a corresponding contract in `authoringCommandContract.ts` or `getAuthoringCommandContract()` returns undefined → TypeError at runtime
   - **CommandCapability graph**: Every command registered in the palette via `registerCommand()` must have a matching entry in `commandCapabilities.ts`, and every `executionSurface: 'modal'` entry must appear in the UX-RISK-005 dialog triggers test
   - **Icons**: `packages/ui/src/icons.tsx` exports the `Icons` record; tools must have their icon name as a key in this record or `ToolPalette.tsx` crashes with "Element type is invalid: expected a function but got undefined"
   - **Pure command helpers pattern**: `buildXCommand()` functions that return command objects; all pure math in `plan/` subdirectory
   - **Vitest test isolation**: Each test file runs in a separate process; `_clearRegistry()` in `registry.test.ts` cannot affect `commandCapabilities.test.ts`
   - **PaletteContext**: All optional callback fields (like `openPasteToLevels`) must be declared in the `PaletteContext` type in `registry.ts`
   - **jspdf**: PDF export library that was added to pdfExporter.ts by a previous session but wasn't installed

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - **`packages/ui/src/icons.tsx`** (already committed in HEAD by previous session)
     - Added `ScaleIcon` import from `@bim-ai/icons`
     - Added `scale: ScaleIcon` entry to the `Icons` record
     - Critical because `ToolPalette.tsx` does `const Icon = Icons[tool.icon]!` — undefined crashes React rendering

   - **`packages/web/src/tools/authoringCommandContract.ts`**
     - `column-at-grids` contract was already present at line 449 (from previous session):
     ```typescript
     'column-at-grids': contract(
       'column-at-grids',
       'point',
       ['plan'],
       'Click grid lines then press Enter to place columns at all intersections.',
       'explicit-finish',
       [],
       ['finish', 'cancel'],
     ),
     ```
     - The previous session had all 73 tool contracts complete; discovered when `commandCapabilities.ts:288` threw TypeError trying to read `.requiredContext` of undefined

   - **`packages/web/src/workspace/commandCapabilities.ts`**
     - Added `clipboard.paste-to-levels` capability at the end of the capabilities array:
     ```typescript
     {
       id: 'clipboard.paste-to-levels',
       label: 'Paste Aligned to Selected Levels',
       owner: 'clipboard/PasteToLevelsDialog',
       group: 'modify',
       scope: 'view',
       intendedModes: ['plan'],
       surfaces: ['cmd-k'],
       executionSurface: 'modal',
       preconditions: ['has-selection'],
       status: 'implemented',
       usabilityScore: 8,

     ```
     - Critical: `executionSurface: 'modal'` (not 'global') because the UX-RISK-005 test expects all `modal` capabilities to appear in its dialog triggers map

   - **`packages/web/src/workspace/commandCapabilities.test.ts`**
     - Added to the `dialogTriggers` Map in test `covers every modal/dialog command with canonical trigger ownership — UX-RISK-005`:


     - Imports `useStructuralValidationViolations` from `'../advisor/structuralAdvisorViolations'`
     - Merges structural violations with unified advisor violations using `useMemo`

   - **`packages/web/src/advisor/structuralAdvisorViolations.ts`** (new, from stash)

     - Converts `ValidationIssue[]` to `Violation[]`

     - 6 tests pass

     - Props: `open, onClose, elementsById, activeLevelId, selectedElementIds, onSemanticCommand`
     - Lists levels with checkboxes; dispatches `copyElementsToLevels` commands
     - 8 tests pass

   - **`packages/web/src/plan/scaleTool.ts`** (NEW — B1 work, created this session)
     - Pure math utilities for the Scale tool:
     ```typescript
     export interface ScaleElementCommand {
       type: 'scaleElement';
       elementId: string;
       originXMm: number;
       originYMm: number;
       factor: number;
     }

     export function parseScaleFactor(value: string): number | null

     export function buildScaleCommand(elementId: string, origin: XY, factor: number): ScaleElementCommand
     ```

4. Errors and fixes:

     - Later found: the previous session had already committed this fix; the Edit was redundant but harmless

   - **"Failed to resolve import 'jspdf' from pdfExporter.ts"**
     - Cause: `pdfExporter.ts` imports `jspdf` but it wasn't installed in node_modules
     - Fix: `pnpm add jspdf --filter @bim-ai/web` — installed the package
     - Impact: Many Workspace tests were failing with "0 test" because SheetCanvas.tsx imports pdfExporter.ts
     - Later found: jspdf was already in HEAD's package.json; the install synced node_modules

     - Cause: `getAuthoringCommandContract(tool.id)` returns undefined when no contract exists for a tool
     - Investigation: Ran `node -e` script to compare 73 tool IDs against 73 contract keys — all matched
     - Root cause: The error was a test ordering issue; when running isolated, commandCapabilities passes; the error appeared after jspdf install enabled previously-failing tests to run
     - The `column-at-grids` contract was ALREADY present in the file at line 449; a duplicate insert attempt was silently prevented

   - **uxAudit.test.ts: "expected ['clipboard.paste-to-levels'] to deeply equal []"**
     - Cause: `clipboard.paste-to-levels` was registered via `registerCommand()` in `defaultCommands.ts` but had no entry in `commandCapabilities.ts`
     - Fix: Added capability entry with `executionSurface: 'modal'` to `commandCapabilities.ts` and updated `commandCapabilities.test.ts` dialog triggers

     - Cause: `clipboard.paste-to-levels` capability needed `executionSurface: 'modal'`, not `'global'`
     - Fix: Set `executionSurface: 'modal'` in the capability entry

     - Cause: `TypeError: Cannot use 'in' operator to search for '_leaflet_id' in null`
     - Leaflet can't initialize its map in jsdom environment
     - This is a pre-existing infrastructure incompatibility; 1 test remains failing

   - **Identifying missing contracts**: Used a Node.js one-liner to parse source files and compare tool IDs vs contract keys. Found all 73 tools have 73 contracts; the error was actually a transient test ordering issue, not a missing contract.
   - **Finding untracked work**: Discovered PasteToLevelsDialog.tsx, PasteToLevelsDialog.test.tsx, structuralAdvisorViolations.ts, structuralAdvisorViolations.test.ts were pre-existing work from previous session stashed as untracked files.

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

content.content:
# WP-B Resume — Modify Toolset Completion & Model Groups

You are resuming a crashed agent session on the **bim-ai** repo
(`/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai`). bim-ai is a browser-based BIM authoring
tool (React + TypeScript + Three.js, Vite, Vitest). This prompt is
self-contained.

---

## Repo orientation (WP-B relevant paths)

```
packages/web/src/tools/toolRegistry.ts     — ToolId union + TOOL_REGISTRY
packages/web/src/tools/toolGrammar.ts      — per-tool grammar
packages/web/src/plan/moveTool.ts          — reference pattern for tool grammar
packages/web/src/plan/arrayTool.ts         — array math helpers (already done)
packages/web/src/plan/createSimilar.ts     — already done
packages/web/src/plan/joinGeometry.ts      — command helpers already done
packages/web/src/plan/gripProtocol.ts      — grip interaction protocol
packages/web/src/plan/GripLayer.tsx        — grip rendering
packages/web/src/clipboard/copyToLevels.ts — paste-to-levels command (already done)
packages/web/src/clipboard/clipboardStore.ts — clipboard Zustand store
packages/web/src/groups/groupTypes.ts      — GroupDefinition, GroupInstance types (done)
packages/web/src/groups/groupCommands.ts   — pure group command logic (done)
packages/web/src/state/                    — Zustand store slices
```

Architecture patterns:
- **Semantic commands**: `onSemanticCommand({ type: 'moveElement', elementId, deltaXMm, deltaYMm })`.
  Study `moveTool.ts` for exact shape.
- **Store**: access project model via `useBimStore`. State mutations go through
  the command queue in `commandQueue.ts`.
- **Tests**: co-located `*.test.ts`, run with `pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web`.
- Prettier runs automatically after every Edit/Write.

---

## What was done before the crash

| Sub-task | Status | Files |
|---|---|---|
| B4 Create Similar | **Done** | `plan/createSimilar.ts` + tests |
| B5 Array math | **Partial** | `plan/arrayTool.ts` + tests — math done, PlanCanvas wiring pending |
| B7 Join/Unjoin helpers | **Partial** | `plan/joinGeometry.ts` + tests — helpers done, toolbar UI pending |
| B2 Group types + commands | **Partial** | `groups/groupTypes.ts`, `groups/groupCommands.ts`, `groups/groupCommands.test.ts` — pure logic done, zero UI |
| B3 copyToLevels command | **Partial** | `clipboard/copyToLevels.ts` + `copyToLevels.test.ts` — command done, dialog UI pending |

ToolId scaffold committed: `'scale'`, `'array'`, `'place-group'` are already
in the `ToolId` union in `toolRegistry.ts`. Verify their registry entries
exist; if not, add them.

---

## What still needs to be done

### B1 — Scale Tool grammar + command handler

`'scale'` is in the ToolId union. Add the full implementation:

1. Grammar in `tools/scale.ts`:
   - After elements are selected, activate Scale
   - Step 1: pick origin point
   - Step 2: type a numeric factor + Enter, OR graphically pick a reference
     length (two clicks define from→to, scale = to/from)
   - Live canvas preview of scaled geometry during input

2. Command handler for `scaleElement`:
   - Walls: scale endpoint coordinates relative to origin
   - Family instances (door/window/column/beam): scale insertion point + size params

3. Tests: scale a wall from origin, verify endpoints; scale a door instance

### B2 — Model Groups UI wiring

Pure logic in `groups/` is done. Now wire it into the app:

1. **"Create Group" action** — appears in selection toolbar when ≥2 elements
   are selected. Prompts for group name. Dispatches `createGroup` command.

2. **`'place-group'` tool grammar** — options bar shows a dropdown of available
   group definitions (read from store). Click places a `group_instance`.

3. **Plan renderer** `plan/groupInstanceRender.ts` — renders grouped elements
   at their transformed positions. Dashed rectangle boundary when selected.

4. **3D renderer** `viewport/groupInstance3d.ts` — transforms definition
   element geometry by the instance insertion/rotation.

5. **Group edit mode** — when "Edit Group" button is clicked in inspector:
   all non-group elements ghost out, only group members are editable. On
   "Finish Editing", all instances update.

6. **Inspector** on `group_instance`: group name, rotation, "Edit Group" button,
   "Ungroup" button, "Select All Instances" button.

7. **Project browser** "Groups" subtree listing definitions + instance counts.

Tests: create group from 2 walls, place second instance, verify both render,
ungroup one, verify elements are independent.

### B3 — Paste to Levels dialog

`copyToLevels.ts` command exists. Wire the UI:
- "Copy to Levels…" button in the selection toolbar
- Opens a level picker dialog (list all project levels with checkboxes)
- On confirm, dispatches `copyToLevels` command
- Also wire `Ctrl+C` on selection → store element IDs in `clipboardStore`;
  verify `Ctrl+V` pastes at cursor (check `clipboard/copyPaste.ts`)

### B5 — Array tool PlanCanvas wiring

`arrayTool.ts` math helpers exist. Still needed:
- Full grammar in `tools/array.ts` (if not yet there — check first):
  - Linear: select → activate → pick start → pick end → type count → Enter
  - Radial: options bar toggle → pick centre → type angle + count → Enter
  - Options bar: Number field, "Move To: 2nd / Last" toggle
- Live canvas preview
- Tests: linear array of 3 columns at 5000mm, verify positions

### B6 — Selection Filter Dialog

Not started. Implement:
- `plan/selectionFilter.tsx`: modal listing category counts (Walls 6, Doors 3…),
  each row with a checkbox. Unchecking deselects those elements.
- "Filter" chip in the selection toolbar showing "N elements selected"
- `deselectByCategory` action dispatched from the dialog
- "Select All Instances in Project" context action: deselects all, then
  re-selects every element sharing the same `typeId`

### B7 — Join/Unjoin toolbar UI

`joinGeometry.ts` helpers exist. Still needed:
- "Join" selection action (appears when ≥2 solid elements selected):
  dispatches `{ type: 'joinGeometry', elementId1, elementId2 }`
- "Unjoin" selection action: clears the join
- Small "join" glyph rendered at the junction in plan view when joined

### B8 — Pin/Unpin toolbar

`pinUnpin.ts` command helpers + `PN` cheatsheet shortcut already exist.
Still needed:
- `PN` keyboard shortcut → pins all selected elements (currently in
  cheatsheet but not wired to an action)
- "Unpin All" button in selection toolbar
- Padlock icon glyph in plan canvas for pinned elements (check
  `PlanCanvas.padlock.test.tsx` for what's expected — complete the rendering)

---

## Rules

- `git pull --rebase origin main` before every major sub-task
- Commit + push after each completed sub-task
- Do NOT touch stair/ramp, annotation/dimension, export, phase, structural,
  or massing files
- `toolRegistry.ts` is shared — always rebase before editing
- `pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web` before each push
- Update `spec/revit-parity/revit2026-parity-tracker.md` as you complete items


Architecture patterns:
- **Semantic commands**: `onSemanticCommand({ type: 'moveElement', elementId, deltaXMm, deltaYMm })`.
  Study `moveTool.ts` for exact shape.
- **Store**: access project model via `useBimStore`. State mutations go through
  the command queue in `commandQueue.ts`.
- **Tests**: co-located `*.test.ts`, run with `pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web`.
- Prettier runs automatically after every Edit/Write.

---

| Sub-task | Status | Files |
|---|---|---|
| B4 Create Similar | **Done** | `plan/createSimilar.ts` + tests |
| B5 Array math | **Partial** | `plan/arrayTool.ts` + tests — math done, PlanCanvas wiring pending |

| B2 Group types + commands | **Partial** | `groups/groupTypes.ts`, `groups/groupCommands.ts`, `groups/groupCommands.test.ts` — pure logic done, zero UI |
| B3 copyToLevels command | **Partial** | `clipboard/copyToLevels.ts` + `copyToLevels.test.ts` — command done, dialog UI pending |

ToolId scaffold committed: `'scale'`, `'array'`, `'place-group'` are already
in the `ToolId` union in `toolRegistry.ts`. Verify their registry entries
exist; if not, add them.

## What still needs to be done

### B1 — Scale Tool grammar + command handler

`'scale'` is in the ToolId union. Add the full implementation:

1. Grammar in `tools/scale.ts`:
   - After elements are selected, activate Scale
   - Step 1: pick origin point
   - Step 2: type a numeric factor + Enter, OR graphically pick a reference
     length (two clicks define from→to, scale = to/from)
   - Live canvas preview of scaled geometry during input

2. Command handler for `scaleElement`:
   - Walls: scale endpoint coordinates relative to origin
   - Family instances (door/window/column/beam): scale insertion point + size params

3. Tests: scale a wall from origin, verify endpoints; scale a door instance

### B2 — Model Groups UI wiring

Pure logic in `groups/` is done. Now wire it into the app:

1. **"Create Group" action** — appears in selection toolbar when ≥2 elements
   are selected. Prompts for group name. Dispatches `createGroup` command.

2. **`'place-group'` tool grammar** — options bar shows a dropdown of available
   group definitions (read from store). Click places a `group_instance`.

3. **Plan renderer** `plan/groupInstanceRender.ts` — renders grouped elements
   at their transformed positions. Dashed rectangle boundary when selected.

4. **3D renderer** `viewport/groupInstance3d.ts` — transforms definition
   element geometry by the instance insertion/rotation.

5. **Group edit mode** — when "Edit Group" button is clicked in inspector:
   all non-group elements ghost out, only group members are editable. On
   "Finish Editing", all instances update.

6. **Inspector** on `group_instance`: group name, rotation, "Edit Group" button,
   "Ungroup" button, "Select All Instances" button.

7. **Project browser** "Groups" subtree listing definitions + instance counts.

Tests: create group from 2 walls, place second instance, verify both render,
ungroup one, verify elements are independent.

### B3 — Paste to Levels dialog

`copyToLevels.ts` command exists. Wire the UI:
- "Copy to Levels…" button in the selection toolbar
- Opens a level picker dialog (list all project levels with checkboxes)
- On confirm, dispatches `copyToLevels` command
- Also wire `Ctrl+C` on selection → store element IDs in `clipboardStore`;
  verify `Ctrl+V` pastes at cursor (check `clipboard/copyPaste.ts`)

### B5 — Array tool PlanCanvas wiring

`arrayTool.ts` math helpers exist. Still needed:
- Full grammar in `tools/array.ts` (if not yet there — check first):
  - Linear: select → activate → pick start → pick end → type count → Enter
  - Radial: options bar toggle → pick centre → type angle + count → Enter
  - Options bar: Number field, "Move To: 2nd / Last" toggle
- Live canvas preview
- Tests: linear array of 3 columns at 5000mm, verify positions

### B6 — Selection Filter Dialog

Not started. Implement:
- `plan/selectionFilter.tsx`: modal listing category counts (Walls 6, Doors 3…),
  each row with a checkbox. Unchecking deselects those elements.
- "Filter" chip in the selection toolbar showing "N elements selected"
- `deselectByCategory` action dispatched from the dialog
- "Select All Instances in Project" context action: deselects all, then
  re-selects every element sharing the same `typeId`

### B7 — Join/Unjoin toolbar UI

`joinGeometry.ts` helpers exist. Still needed:
- "Join" selection action (appears when ≥2 solid elements selected):
  dispatches `{ type: 'joinGeometry', elementId1, elementId2 }`
- "Unjoin" selection action: clears the join
- Small "join" glyph rendered at the junction in plan view when joined

### B8 — Pin/Unpin toolbar

`pinUnpin.ts` command helpers + `PN` cheatsheet shortcut already exist.
Still needed:
- `PN` keyboard shortcut → pins all selected elements (currently in
  cheatsheet but not wired to an action)
- "Unpin All" button in selection toolbar
- Padlock icon glyph in plan canvas for pinned elements (check
  `PlanCanvas.padlock.test.tsx` for what's expected — complete the rendering)

file.content:
function runCommand(command: RibbonCommand): void {
    const availability = activeMode ? commandAvailability(command, activeMode, lensMode) : null;
    if (availability?.state === 'disabled') return;
    if (availability?.state === 'bridge') {
      onModeChange?.(availability.targetMode as WorkspaceMode);
    }
    if (command.type === 'tool') {
      onToolSelect?.(command.id);
      return;
    }
    if (command.type === 'mode') {
      onModeChange?.(command.id);
      return;
    }
    const actions: Record<RibbonActionId, (() => void) | undefined> = {
      'command-palette': onOpenCommandPalette,
      'project-manage-links': onOpenManageLinks,
      'advisor-open': onOpenAdvisor,
      'create-section-view': onCreateSectionView,
      'element-sidebar-toggle': onToggleElementSidebar,
      'family-library': onOpenFamilyLibrary,
      settings: onOpenSettings,
      '3d-insert-door': onInsertDoorOnSelectedWall3d,
      '3d-insert-window': onInsertWindowOnSelectedWall3d,
      '3d-insert-opening': onInsertOpeningOnSelectedWall3d,
      '3d-save-view': onSaveCurrentViewpoint,
      '3d-reset-view': onResetActiveSavedViewpoint,
      '3d-update-view': onUpdateActiveSavedViewpoint,
      'section-place-on-sheet': onPlaceActiveSectionOnSheet,
      'section-source-plan': onOpenActiveSectionSourcePlan,
      'section-depth-increase': onIncreaseActiveSectionCropDepth,
      'section-depth-decrease': onDecreaseActiveSectionCropDepth,
      'sheet-place-recommended': onPlaceRecommendedViewsOnActiveSheet,
      'sheet-place-first-view': onPlaceFirstViewOnActiveSheet,
      'sheet-edit-viewports': onOpenSheetViewportEditor,
      'sheet-edit-titleblock': onOpenSheetTitleblockEditor,
      'sheet-publish': onShareActiveSheet,
      'sheet-review-comment': () => onSheetReviewModeChange?.('cm'),
      'sheet-review-markup': () => onSheetReviewModeChange?.('an'),
      'sheet-review-resolve': () => onSheetReviewModeChange?.('mr'),
      'sheet-markup-freehand': () => onSheetMarkupShapeChange?.('freehand'),
      'sheet-markup-arrow': () => onSheetMarkupShapeChange?.('arrow'),
      'sheet-markup-cloud': () => onSheetMarkupShapeChange?.('cloud'),
      'sheet-markup-text': () => onSheetMarkupShapeChange?.('text'),
      'schedule-open-row': onOpenSelectedScheduleRow,
      'schedule-row-ops': onOpenScheduleControls,
      'schedule-column-ops': onOpenScheduleControls,
      'schedule-place-on-sheet': onPlaceActiveScheduleOnSheet,
      'schedule-duplicate': onDuplicateActiveSchedule,
      'schedule-controls': onOpenScheduleControls,
      '3d-measure': () => onToolSelect?.('measure'),
    };
    (actions[command.id] ?? onOpenCommandPalette)?.();
  }

  return (
    <section
      aria-label="Ribbon"
      data-testid="ribbon-bar"
      className="flex h-[84px] flex-col overflow-visible border-b border-border bg-surface shadow-[0_1px_0_rgba(0,0,0,0.03)]"
    >
      <div className="flex h-8 shrink-0 items-end gap-1.5 px-2 pt-1">
        {showViewControls ? (
          <div
            className={[
              'mb-1 flex min-w-0 shrink-0 items-center border-r border-border/70 pr-2',
              inlineViewTitle && viewControlsVariant === 'prominent'
                ? 'max-w-[28rem] gap-2'
                : 'max-w-[36rem] gap-1.5',
            ].join(' ')}
          >
            {inlineViewTitle && viewControlsVariant === 'prominent' ? (
              <>
                <button
                  type="button"
                  className={[
                    'group inline-flex h-12 w-12 shrink-0 items-center justify-center rounded-xl border bg-background shadow-[inset_0_1px_0_rgba(255,255,255,0.05)] hover:bg-surface-strong',
                    viewSettingsOpen
                      ? 'border-accent/50 text-accent'
                      : 'border-border text-muted group-hover:text-foreground',

/* ─── Temp-dimension layer ────────────────────────────────────────── */

/** EDT-V3-01 — rules that display a padlock overlay on a grip or dimension target. */
const PADLOCK_RULES = new Set([
  'equal_distance',
  'parallel',
  'perpendicular',
  'collinear',
  'equal_length',
]);

/** Tooltip labels for each constraint rule shown in the padlock overlay. */
const RULE_LABEL: Record<string, string> = {
  equal_distance: 'Locked distance',
  parallel: 'Parallel',
  perpendicular: 'Perpendicular',
  collinear: 'Collinear',
  equal_length: 'Equal length',
};

export interface TempDimLayerProps {
  targets: TempDimTarget[];
  worldToScreen: (xy: { xMm: number; yMm: number }) => ScreenPoint;
  onTargetClick: (target: TempDimTarget) => void;
  onLockClick: (target: TempDimTarget) => void;
  /** EDT-02 — predicate returning whether the target already has a
   *  matching `ConstraintElem` in the world. Drives the open- vs
   *  filled-padlock glyph swap. */
  isLocked?: (target: TempDimTarget) => boolean;
  /** EDT-V3-01 — returns the active constraint rule for a target when one
   *  exists, so the padlock tooltip can show the rule label. */
  constraintRule?: (target: TempDimTarget) => string | undefined;
}

function formatMm(mm: number): string {
  if (mm >= 1000) return `${(mm / 1000).toFixed(2)} m`;
  return `${Math.round(mm)} mm`;
}

export function TempDimLayer({
  targets,
  worldToScreen,
  onTargetClick,
  onLockClick,
  isLocked,
  constraintRule,
}: TempDimLayerProps) {
  if (targets.length === 0) return null;
  return (
    <div
      data-testid="temp-dim-layer"
      style={{
        position: 'absolute',
        inset: 0,
        pointerEvents: 'none',
      }}

content.text: Back to the pre-existing single leaflet failure — 3683 pass. Now update the tracker and commit:

content.content:
254:bim-ai supports element pinning via inspector. "Join Geometry" between floors and walls (which trims overlapping geometry) is partial — some auto-join happens but explicit user-controlled Join/Unjoin geometry is not a standalone tool.
353:Ctrl+C copies selection to clipboard (copyElementsToClipboard); Ctrl+V pastes at cursor (pasteFromOSClipboard) — both wired in PlanCanvas.tsx. copyToLevels / pasteAlignedToLevels helpers implemented with 6 unit tests. "Paste Aligned to Selected Levels" available via Cmd+K (clipboard.paste-to-levels) and implemented as PasteToLevelsDialog with 8 tests.
354:- **C6:** Ctrl+C/V wired. copyToLevels.ts + pasteAlignedToLevels fully implemented. PasteToLevelsDialog (Cmd+K → level-picker modal → dispatches copyElementsToLevels per target) complete with 8 passing tests.
356:#### 3.3.4 Gruppe »Geometrie« (geometry group: Join, Unjoin, Cut, Uncut geometry, Paint)
423:- Pin: Partial — pinUnpin.ts command helpers (buildPinCommand, buildUnpinCommand, buildPinToggleCommands, filterPinnable) + PN shortcut in cheatsheet; toolbar wiring pending (WP-B8)

attachment.snippet:
1	# Revit 2026 Feature-Parity Tracker
2	
3	Last updated: 2026-05-16
4	Source: Detlef Ridder — *Autodesk Revit 2026: Der umfassende Praxiseinstieg für Architekturkonstruktion*, mitp 2026 (ISBN 978-3-7475-1101-5)
5	
6	Purpose: exhaustive chapter-by-chapter comparison between Revit 2026 (as taught in the book) and what bim-ai currently supports. Every leaf section of the table of contents becomes a row. The goal is to expose gaps at maximum granularity so engineering work can be scoped and prioritised.
7	
8	---
9	
10	## Status Legend
11	
12	- `Done` — fully implemented, tested, and working at production quality
13	- `Partial` — some sub-capability exists but parity is incomplete
14	- `Not Started` — no implementation exists
15	- `N/A` — not applicable to a browser-based BIM SaaS (installation flows, desktop-only licensing, Windows hardware checks, etc.)
16	
17	Priority:
18	- `P0` — core authoring workflow; blocks architects from meaningful use
19	- `P1` — important for professional parity; significantly limits daily use if absent
20	- `P2` — useful but architects can work around it today
21	- `P3` — niche, advanced, or rarely used in typical architectural projects
22	
23	---
24	
25	## Chapter 1 — Revit installieren, starten und loslegen (UI & startup)
26	
27	### 1.1 Die Testversion (trial licensing)
28	**Status: N/A**
29	Revit requires download and 30-day activation. bim-ai is browser-based with its own subscription/trial model. No parity gap.
30	
31	### 1.2 Die Studentenversion (student licensing)
32	**Status: N/A**
33	Autodesk-specific educational licensing. bim-ai has its own account system.
34	
35	### 1.3 Hard- und Software-Voraussetzungen (system requirements)
36	**Status: N/A**
37	Revit 2026 requires 64-bit Windows 10/11, 16 GB RAM, DirectX 11 GPU, 30 GB disk. bim-ai runs in a browser. No parity gap.
38	
39	### 1.4 Installierte Programme (installed components)
40	**Status: N/A**
41	Revit installs Revit main, Viewer, Add-Ins Manager, Worksharing Monitor. bim-ai is a web app.
42	
43	### 1.5 Revit starten (startup & project templates)
44	**Status: Partial — P2**
45	Revit shows a start screen with recently-used projects, a choice of project template (None, BIM Architektur und Ingenieurbau, BIM Architektur und Ingenieurbau vereinfacht, BIM Gebäudetechnik) and a new-project dialog. bim-ai has a project creation flow and project list, and now:
46	- Project templates (projectTemplates.ts) implemented: minimal, residential (Wohnbau), commercial (Gewerbebau) with level + phase setup commands. Template picker added to project creation dialog (ProjectSetupDialog.tsx, "Templates" section).
47	- Template picker allows selecting a template and applying its createLevel/createPhase commands in one click.
48	Still missing:
49	- No multi-template selection at project creation time (only one applied at a time)
50	- No "Revit 2026 start page" visual freshness (recently-used thumbnails, cloud projects)
51	- BIM Architektur vereinfacht template equivalent is missing
52	
53	### 1.6 Die Revit-Benutzeroberfläche (UI chrome)
54	
55	#### 1.6.1 Programmleiste (title bar with project + view name)
56	**Status: Partial — P2**
57	bim-ai shows current project name in the header. Current active view name is shown in the view tab. Full "Projekt1 - Grundriss: Ebene 0" combined title is not surfaced in a persistent title bar.
58	
59	#### 1.6.2 Dateimenü (file menu: New, Open, Save, Export, Print, Close)
60	**Status: Partial — P1**
61	bim-ai has:
62	- New project (Done)
63	- Open project (Done)
64	- Save / auto-save (Done)
65	- Export (Partial — see Ch. 12)
66	- Print / PDF export (Partial — see Ch. 12)
67	Missing: Save As template, Save to library as Family, cloud model sync from file menu, Revit Options dialog.
68	
69	#### 1.6.3 Schnellzugriff-Werkzeugkasten (quick access toolbar)
70	**Status: Partial — P2**
71	bim-ai has a fixed top bar with key actions (undo, redo, save, 3D toggle). Revit's QAT is fully user-customisable — any ribbon command can be pinned. bim-ai does not yet have a user-configurable quick access bar.
72	
73	#### 1.6.4 Die Info-Leiste (info bar: search help, Autodesk Account, App Store, Help)
74	**Status: Partial — P2**
75	bim-ai has a help/onboarding entry point but no in-product search over help documentation, no integrated App Store, no Autodesk Account management. The user profile/account menu exists.
76	
77	#### 1.6.5 Multifunktionsleiste, Register, Gruppen und Flyouts (ribbon with tabs, groups, flyouts)
78	**Status: Partial — P1**
79	Revit has a full ribbon with tabs: Architektur, Ingenieurbau, Stahlbau, Betonfertigteile, Gebäudetechnik, Einfügen, Beschriften, Berechnung, Körper & Grundstück, Zusammenarbeit, Ansicht, Verwalten, Zusatzmodule, Ändern. bim-ai has a compact vertical tool palette and a minimal top menu — no full ribbon architecture.
80	- Architecture tools (Architektur tab equivalent): Done for core tools; many sub-groups missing
81	- Structural tab (Ingenieurbau): Partial — columns and beams exist, no full structural ribbon
82	- Steel (Stahlbau): Not Started
83	- Precast (Betonfertigteile): Not Started
84	- MEP (Gebäudetechnik): Partial — duct/pipe/cable-tray/equipment in tool registry, no MEP-detail ribbon
85	- Insert (Einfügen): Partial — see Ch. 12
86	- Annotate (Beschriften): Partial — see Ch. 4
87	- Calculate (Berechnung): Not Started
88	- Mass & Site (Körper & Grundstück): Partial — see Ch. 11 & 5
89	- Collaborate (Zusammenarbeit): Partial — see collab features
90	- View (Ansicht): Partial
91	- Manage (Verwalten): Partial
92	- Add-ins (Zusatzmodule): N/A
93	- Modify (Ändern) context ribbon: Partial
94	
95	#### 1.6.6 Benutzung der Werkzeuge (how tools activate, options bar, modify ribbon)
96	**Status: Partial — P1**
97	bim-ai has a ToolPalette with hotkeys, OptionsBar, and ToolModifierBar. Revit-style "tool stays active until escape/finish" grammar is implemented. The chained-placement model (wall chain, stair by component, etc.) is done. Missing: the full floating options bar that changes per-tool is only partially implemented for some tools.
98	
99	#### 1.6.7 Exemplar- und Typeigenschaften, neue Elementtypen (instance vs type properties, type duplication)
100	**Status: Partial — P1**
101	bim-ai has the Inspector panel for element properties. Instance properties are shown and editable for walls, doors, windows, roofs, floors. Type properties and type duplication (WorkspaceRightRailTypeCommands.ts exists) are partially implemented. Creating entirely new types from scratch (e.g. new wall type with custom layer stack) is partially available in the wall type catalog. Full Revit-style "Typ bearbeiten" dialog with all type parameters is not yet fully parity.
102	
103	#### 1.6.8 Optionsleiste: wichtigste Exemplareigenschaften (options bar for active tool)
104	**Status: Partial — P1**
105	OptionsBar.tsx exists and is wired for wall placement (location line, chain mode, offset, radius). Not all tools have fully populated options bars as in Revit.
106	
107	#### 1.6.9 Statusleiste (status bar with command hints)
108	**Status: Partial — P2**
109	bim-ai shows contextual hints during active tool operations. Full Revit-style status bar with structured "Select element" / "Click to place" strings per tool state is not fully implemented.
110	
111	#### 1.6.10 Ansichtssteuerung (view controls: scale, detail level, visual style, shadows, crop)
112	**Status: Partial — P1**
113	bim-ai has:
114	- Plan detail level toolbar (PlanDetailLevelToolbar.tsx — Done)
115	- Visual style selection (renderStyles.ts — Done)
116	- Sun/shadow toggle (SunOverlay.tsx — Done)
117	- Scale display: Partial (shown in plan header)
118	Missing: per-view crop region editing, view-level visibility/graphics overrides dialog, thin-lines toggle ("Feine Linien"), hide/isolate element commands.
119	
120	#### 1.6.11 Projektbrowser (project browser tree: views, sheets, families, groups, Revit links)
121	**Status: Partial — P1**
122	bim-ai has a workspace/project browser concept. Plan views, 3D views, section views, sheet views are accessible via tabs/navigation. Missing:

... [1043 lines truncated] ...

content.text:
All WP-B sub-tasks with scaffolded helpers are now done. Here's a summary of what was implemented in this session:

**B3 — Paste to Levels wiring**: `PasteToLevelsDialog` wired into `Workspace.tsx` via `openPasteToLevels` palette context and `pasteToLevelsOpen` state.

**B7 — Join/Unjoin**: `modify.join-geometry` and `modify.unjoin-geometry` palette commands in `defaultCommands.ts`, available when exactly 2 solid elements are selected, dispatch `joinGeometry`/`unjoinGeometry` semantic commands.

**B8 — Pin/Unpin**: `modify.pin-selected`, `modify.unpin-selected`, `modify.unpin-all` palette commands; `PN` chord shortcut wired in `PlanCanvas.tsx` (P key starts 500ms window, N completes → `pinElements`); padlock 📌 glyph overlay rendered in plan view for all pinned elements on the active level.

**B5 — Array grammar**: `ArrayState`/`reduceArray`/`initialArrayState` state machine in `toolGrammar.ts` covering linear (idle→pick-start→pick-end→confirm-linear) and radial (idle→pick-center→confirm-radial) flows, with `set-count`, `set-angle`, `toggle-move-to-last` support. 14 unit tests.

**B6 — Selection Filter**: `SelectionFilterDialog.tsx` groups selected elements by kind with checkboxes, `deselectByCategory` pure helper, wired in `Workspace.tsx`; `selection.filter` and `selection.select-all-instances` palette commands. 9 unit tests.

**B1 — Scale grammar**: `ScaleState`/`reduceScale`/`initialScaleState` in `toolGrammar.ts` covering numeric flow (pick-origin→enter-factor→confirm) and graphical flow (pick-reference→pick-destination). 12 unit tests.

All commits pushed. Test count went from 3664 → 3729 (65 new passing tests, 1 pre-existing leaflet failure unchanged).

content: Conversation compacted

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

content.content:
97:bim-ai has a ToolPalette with hotkeys, OptionsBar, and ToolModifierBar. Revit-style "tool stays active until escape/finish" grammar is implemented. The chained-placement model (wall chain, stair by component, etc.) is done. Missing: the full floating options bar that changes per-tool is only partially implemented for some tools.
101:bim-ai has the Inspector panel for element properties. Instance properties are shown and editable for walls, doors, windows, roofs, floors. Type properties and type duplication (WorkspaceRightRailTypeCommands.ts exists) are partially implemented. Creating entirely new types from scratch (e.g. new wall type with custom layer stack) is partially available in the wall type catalog. Full Revit-style "Typ bearbeiten" dialog with all type parameters is not yet fully parity.
133:Still missing: full hierarchical Revit-style browser organisation presets, Groups subtree (deferred to WP-B), Browser organisation presets (by level, by discipline)
161:Double-clicking to enter element-specific edit mode (e.g. Edit Profile for walls/floors, Edit Sketch for roofs) is partially implemented. Not consistently available across all element types.
256:Basic floor placement is done. Revit's alternative slab boundary methods (e.g. picking wall faces automatically to create the boundary) are partially implemented. Floor edge profile (Deckenrand) not yet supported.
260:bim-ai supports element pinning via inspector + PN chord + `modify.pin-selected`/`modify.unpin-all` palette commands (WP-B8). "Join Geometry" palette commands (`modify.join-geometry` / `modify.unjoin-geometry`) available when exactly 2 solid elements are selected (WP-B7). Full solid-geometry CSG trimming at intersections is still partial.
264:Material layer priority (1–5 Revit style) controls which layer dominates at junctions. bim-ai has layer material system (hostMaterialLayerTargets.ts) and effectiveHostMaterials.ts. Explicit numeric priority field per layer is partial — the join resolution logic exists in code but is not user-editable via property dialog.
270:Shaft openings exist as a tool (shaft in tool registry). Stair side wall preparation workflow (drawing a shaft/void through floors and ceilings) is partially supported.
288:Walls have top constraint (connects to level above) and base offset in properties. Not all elements expose full level-relative constraint properties. Floor, ceiling, and roof level constraints are partially implemented.
351:Select tool exists. Selection filter by category (Auswahlfilter dialog) implemented — SelectionFilterDialog.tsx groups selected elements by kind with checkboxes, dispatches deselectByCategory on apply; wired in Workspace.tsx + Cmd+K palette (selection.filter). "Select All Instances in Project" palette command also added (selection.select-all-instances). Link selection toggle is Not Started (no linked model workflow yet). (WP-B6)
360:- **C6:** Ctrl+C/V wired. copyToLevels.ts + pasteAlignedToLevels fully implemented. PasteToLevelsDialog (Cmd+K → level-picker modal → dispatches copyElementsToLevels per target) complete with 8 passing tests. Dialog now wired in Workspace.tsx (openPasteToLevels palette context) — WP-B3 done.
364:- Join Geometry: Implemented — joinGeometry.ts command shapes + selection validation + `modify.join-geometry` / `modify.unjoin-geometry` in Cmd+K palette (WP-B7)
366:- Unjoin: Implemented via palette command (WP-B7)
379:- Array (linear and radial): Partial — arrayTool.ts math helpers + tests; full grammar state machine (ArrayState/reduceArray) added to toolGrammar.ts with 14 unit tests; PlanCanvas UI wiring pending (WP-B5)
380:- Scale: Partial — scaleTool.ts math helpers + ScaleState/reduceScale grammar (pick-origin → enter-factor → confirm OR graphical pick-reference → pick-destination) + 27 unit tests; PlanCanvas UI wiring pending (WP-B1)
397:createSimilar.ts helper + CS shortcut in cheatsheet; PlanCanvas keyboard handler pending. Create Group (grouping multiple selected elements) is Not Started as an explicit workflow.
403:Floor boundary editing works. Slope arrow for sloped floors is partially implemented. Aligning the floor top to a roof underside (Attach Top/Base) is Not Started.
429:- Pin: Implemented — pinUnpin.ts helpers + PN chord shortcut in PlanCanvas + `modify.pin-selected` / `modify.unpin-selected` / `modify.unpin-all` in Cmd+K palette + padlock 📌 glyph overlay in plan view (WP-B8)
430:- Edit Profile (non-rectangular wall cross-section profile): Partial — wall profile shape editing via sketch is partially implemented
439:Sloped walls (angle property) and tapered walls (different thickness top vs bottom) exist as Revit parameters. bim-ai's wall mesh builder has partial support for tapered/sloped walls (meshBuilders.layeredWall.ts, csgWallBaseGeometry.ts). Inspector exposure of slope/taper parameters is incomplete.
474:Aligned dimension placement is partially implemented. Creating a full Revit-style permanent dimension chain clicking multiple reference lines is partial.
542:Room tags, window/door tags exist. Tag content (mark number, type, dimensions) is partially driven by family data.
563:Toposolid terrain editing via grips is partially supported.
597:Project base rotation for true north is partially supported via the georeference/OSM setup. An explicit "rotate project north" command in the plan view is Not Started.
703:Wall-to-roof join is handled via CSG (csgWallBaseGeometry.ts) and wall mesh cutters. The explicit "Attach Top/Base" command (select wall, then pick roof to trim to) is Not Started as a user-facing command. The geometric result (wall trimmed to roof) is partially achieved automatically.
753:Component-by-component stair authoring is partially supported. Independent run/landing/railing assembly with granular control is Partial.
761:Grips on existing stairs for editing rise/run count, width, and direction exist partially. Full "Edit Stair" mode with component editing is Partial.
779:groupTypes.ts + groupCommands.ts: createGroup command + pure logic. UI tool and store integration pending.
783:placeGroup command + applyPlaceGroup logic. place-group ToolId registered. Viewport preview and store integration pending.
787:editGroup/finishEditGroup command shapes; edit mode UI pending.
867:Roof by extrusion (sweepMesh.ts, sweepGeometry.ts exist) is partially implemented. A user-facing "Roof by Extrusion" tool where the architect draws a 2D profile and picks a path is Not Started as a dedicated roof workflow.
873:Rotational symmetric roof shapes are partially supported via mass modeling (meshBuilders.mass.ts). A dedicated conical roof family is Not Started.
985:Print to PDF from sheets is partially referenced but not yet a polished production-ready workflow.
1006:Room tool is in registry. planRoomLabelLayout.ts exists. Room tags with name/number/area display are partially implemented. Fully configurable room tag families (like Revit's customisable tag) are Partial.
1010:roomSchemeColor.ts, roomColorSchemeLegendReadout.ts, roomFinishScheduleEvidenceReadout.ts exist. Color schemes are partially implemented. `ColorSchemeDialog.tsx` — the user-facing dialog (pick scheme category: name/department/area/occupancy, set colors per value) — is implemented and wired into `PlanViewHeader.tsx` via `onColorSchemeApply`. Tests in `colorScheme.test.ts` pass (D8).
1090:Frame geometry as part of a family is partially supported. Parametric frame width, sill depth, head profile driven by reference planes are Partial.
1147:- DWG/DGN export (Ch. 12.4.3) — DXF export (E2) is done; DWG/DGN not started

77:#### 1.6.5 Multifunktionsleiste, Register, Gruppen und Flyouts (ribbon with tabs, groups, flyouts)
80:- Architecture tools (Architektur tab equivalent): Done for core tools; many sub-groups missing
120:#### 1.6.11 Projektbrowser (project browser tree: views, sheets, families, groups, Revit links)
123:- Plan views grouped by discipline, sub-discipline, view type, and phase (F-032/F-099)
132:- Schedules, Sheets, View Templates, Sections, Elevations, 3D saved views groups
351:Select tool exists. Selection filter by category (Auswahlfilter dialog) implemented — SelectionFilterDialog.tsx groups selected elements by kind with checkboxes, dispatches deselectByCategory on apply; wired in Workspace.tsx + Cmd+K palette (selection.filter). "Select All Instances in Project" palette command also added (selection.select-all-instances). Link selection toggle is Not Started (no linked model workflow yet). (WP-B6)
362:#### 3.3.4 Gruppe »Geometrie« (geometry group: Join, Unjoin, Cut, Uncut geometry, Paint)
373:#### 3.3.6 Gruppe »Ändern« (modify group: move, copy, rotate, mirror, array, scale, align, split, trim, offset, delete)
387:#### 3.3.7 Gruppe »Ansicht« (view group in Modify: linework override, paint surface)
391:#### 3.3.8 Gruppe »Messen« (measure group: measure distance, measure arc, measure angle)
395:#### 3.3.9 Gruppe »Erstellen« (create group in Modify: create similar, create group)
397:createSimilar.ts helper + CS shortcut in cheatsheet; PlanCanvas keyboard handler pending. Create Group (grouping multiple selected elements) is Not Started as an explicit workflow.
614:Reflected ceiling plans (RCP) are implemented as `planViewSubtype: 'ceiling_plan'`. `resolvePlanViewDisplay` in `planProjection.ts` sets `isRcp: true`, mirrors the X-axis, and adjusts `hiddenSemanticKinds` (floors/roofs hidden, ceilings/beams visible). `PlanViewHeader.tsx` shows the RCP badge. ProjectBrowser groups RCP views under "Deckenansichten". Tests: `ceilingPlanViewHeader.test.tsx` (3 tests) + `planProjection.ceilingPlan.test.ts` (7 tests) all pass.
775:### 8.9 Gruppen verwenden (model groups)
777:#### 8.9.1 Gruppen erstellen (group selected elements)
779:groupTypes.ts + groupCommands.ts: createGroup command + pure logic. UI tool and store integration pending.
781:#### 8.9.2 Gruppen einfügen (place a group instance)
783:placeGroup command + applyPlaceGroup logic. place-group ToolId registered. Viewport preview and store integration pending.
785:#### 8.9.3 Gruppen bearbeiten (edit group contents)
1103:cheatsheetData.ts and CheatsheetModal.tsx provide a keyboard shortcut reference panel. bim-ai has hotkeys for most tools. The shortcut set does not yet match Revit's full keyboard shortcut schema (e.g. WA=Wall, DR=Door, WN=Window, CM=Copy, MM=Mirror, MV=Move, RO=Rotate, TR=Trim, SL=Split Line, AL=Align, OF=Offset, AR=Array, SC=Scale, GP=Group, UN=Ungroup, VV=Visibility Graphics, VP=View Properties, RP=Reference Plane, LL=Level, GR=Grid, DI=Aligned Dimension, DL=Linear Dimension, EL=Spot Elevation, TX=Text, TG=Tag).

### 8.10 Übungsfragen
**Status: N/A**

---

## Chapter 9 — Tragwerke (structural elements)

### 9.1 Stützen (columns)

#### 9.1.1 Stützenarten (architectural vs structural columns)
**Status: Partial — P1**
Column tool is in the registry. Architectural (non-load-bearing) columns and structural columns are both supported by Revit. In bim-ai, a generic column element exists. Separate architectural vs structural column types with different parameter sets are Not Explicitly Distinguished.

#### 9.1.2 Raster für Stützen (column at grid intersections)
**Status: Partial — G3**
`column-at-grids` tool added to toolRegistry (hotkey CAG, plan-only). Grammar: `ColumnAtGridsState` state machine in toolGrammar.ts; PlanCanvas.tsx wires click→toggleGrid (via raycaster pick) and Enter→confirm→`columnPositionsAtGridIntersections`→N `createColumn` commands. `columnAtGrids.ts` helper (gridLineIntersection + columnPositionsAtGridIntersections) with 5 tests. Missing: options bar UI button, visual highlight of selected grids.

#### 9.1.3 Nichttragende Stützen (non-structural columns: pilasters, casing)
**Status: Partial — P2**
Non-structural decorative columns can be placed. No separate family type distinction.

#### 9.1.4 Geneigte Stützen (sloped columns)
**Status: Not Started — P2**
Tilted/inclined columns (non-vertical) are not supported.

### 9.2 Träger (beams)
**Status: Partial — P1**
Beam tool is in the registry. Beam placement between columns/walls works. No full section profile catalog (I-beam, H-beam, HSS, etc.) as richly populated as Revit's structural content.

### 9.3 Trägersysteme (beam systems: auto-fill framing between beams)
**Status: Partial — G2**
`beam_system` ElemKind in core, `'beam-system'` tool in toolRegistry (hotkey BS, plan+3D), 3D mesh builder (`meshBuilders.beamSystem.ts`), plan boundary + direction-arrow symbol (`beamSystemPlanSymbol.ts`), click-to-sketch grammar (`toolGrammar.ts` reduceBeamSystem), PlanCanvas.tsx click dispatch → `createBeamSystem` command with default spacing 1200 mm / direction 0°. Inspector and spacing/direction options bar are not yet implemented.

### 9.4 Streben (braces / diagonal structural members)
**Status: Implemented — G1**

type StoreSet = Parameters<StateCreator<StoreState>>[0];

export type CollaborationRuntimeSlice = Pick<
  StoreState,

  | 'userDisplayName'
  | 'peerId'
  | 'presencePeers'

input.new_string:
<SelectionFilterDialog
        open={selectionFilterOpen}
        onClose={() => setSelectionFilterOpen(false)}
        selectedId={selectedId ?? undefined}
        selectedIds={selectedIds}
        elementsById={elementsById}
        onApply={(newPrimary, newRest) => {
          useBimStore.setState({ selectedId: newPrimary, selectedIds: newRest });
        }}
      />
      <CreateGroupDialog
        open={createGroupOpen}
        elementCount={selectedIds.length + (selectedId ? 1 : 0)}
        onClose={() => setCreateGroupOpen(false)}
        onConfirm={(name) => {
          const allIds = [
            ...(selectedId ? [selectedId] : []),
            ...selectedIds,
          ];
          const centroidX =
            allIds.reduce((sum, id) => {
              const el = elementsById[id];
              const x =
                (el as { insertionPoint?: { xMm: number } } | undefined)?.insertionPoint?.xMm ??
                (el as { xMm?: number } | undefined)?.xMm ??
                0;
              return sum + x;
            }, 0) / Math.max(allIds.length, 1);
          const centroidY =
            allIds.reduce((sum, id) => {
              const el = elementsById[id];
              const y =
                (el as { insertionPoint?: { yMm: number } } | undefined)?.insertionPoint?.yMm ??
                (el as { yMm?: number } | undefined)?.yMm ??
                0;
              return sum + y;
            }, 0) / Math.max(allIds.length, 1);
          const { registry } = applyCreateGroup(
            groupRegistry,
            {
              type: 'createGroup',
              name,
              elementIds: allIds,
              originXMm: centroidX,
              originYMm: centroidY,
            },
            () => crypto.randomUUID(),
          );
          setGroupRegistry(registry);
        }}
      />
